This suggests that clicking the top blue block didn't move the pair but changed the white regions. Maybe the blue blocks control something else in this level.

But the pair didn't move when I clicked the top blue. Maybe the pair can't move up from its current position because there's a blocker?

- Bottom blue → LEFT
So in Level 2, maybe the top blue also moves RIGHT. But the pair didn't move. Let me check if the pair actually didn't move or if I'm misreading the data.
Actually, I think the issue is that the pair CAN'T move right because there's no white region to move into. In Level 1, the pair moved within the white region. Here, the pair is in the dark gray region, and there's no white region to the right.
Wait, but the white regions changed. The top white region disappeared and the middle white region shrunk. Maybe the blue blocks control the white regions, not the pair directly. And the pair moves as a side effect of the white region changing.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Click the bottom blue block (rows 44-47, cols 0-3)
predict(expect_change=True, note='Testing bottom blue block - expect pair to move or white regions to change')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 45, 'col': 2}])

print("Result:", last_action_result)

seg = current_frame.segmentation

print("\n=== SMALL OBJECTS ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] < 500:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)} hash={n['hash']}")

# Also check large objects
print("\n=== LARGE OBJECTS ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] >= 500:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)} hash={n['hash']}")

if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('done'):
    print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
else:
    print("Level not yet complete")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
